Tool Call: retrieve("Indian Penal Code Sections 228A, 222A, 224A, 226A")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

Based on reviewing the newly gathered evidence, there appears to be no direct reference to sections of the Indian Penal Code (IPC) pertaining to disclosure restrictions for victims of rape. The evidence instead focuses primarily on regulatory statutes governing medical practitioners' licensing processes and responsibilities rather than penal codes. Given my initial agreement with option A (Section 228A), since it directly relates to protecting privacy and confidentiality of rape victims, I stand firm on this position despite the lack of explicit mention in the retrieved material.
